audit_title: Dashboard Data Architecture Audit - Critical Issues for Web Extension
scope: Identify metrics calculated in dashboard vs pre-calculated in tables; flag architectural issues for web version extension

executive_summary.architectural_debt: SIGNIFICANT - Performance metrics calculated in dashboard should be pre-calculated in dedicated tables for web API reuse

risk_if_left_alone: Web API cannot return consistent win rate without duplicating dashboard logic
Different calculation in web vs dashboard causes confusion
Backend services must call dashboard code or re-implement logic
Mobile app will have different calculation if built independently
Breakeven handling (lines 1076-1082) creates business logic in presentation layer
findings.recommendation: Pre-calculate win_rate_pct in algo_performance_daily via dedicated loader. Include: win_count, loss_count, breakeven_count, total_trades, wr_pct, wr_confidence_flag.
findings.table_status: algo_performance_daily EXISTS in schema but NO LOADER found; data may be stale or missing
findings.effort: HIGH - requires new loader; fetch_perf() can then read from table instead of computing

risk_if_left_alone: Undefined (None) when losses=0, but no validation prevents division by zero in other callers
Web API clients may not handle None gracefully
Calculation mixed with validation (lines 1134-1141) - hard to separate concerns
Zero-loss scenarios (all winning trades) will return None instead of Infinity/error code
findings.recommendation: Store profit_factor_pct in algo_performance_daily. Handle edge cases explicitly: losses=0 → store as NULL with validation flag; losses<epsilon → flag as unreliable.
findings.table_status: READY in schema, NO DATA (no loader)
findings.effort: MEDIUM - straightforward calc, but edge case handling needed

risk_if_left_alone: DUPLICATION: fetch_perf() calculates expectancy; fetch_perf_analytics() reads from algo_performance_daily
If formula changes in dashboard, table becomes stale; web clients get old values
No synchronization check - dashboard vs table may diverge
Historical backtests vs live trades may use different formulas
findings.recommendation: DELETE expectancy calculation from fetch_perf(). Ensure algo_performance_daily is populated daily by loader. Dashboard reads from table only.
findings.table_status: Column exists in schema (DECIMAL 6,4), but unknown if populated by loader
findings.effort: HIGH - requires loader verification; may need to update 3+ fetch functions

risk_if_left_alone: THREE sources of truth: dashboard calc, portfolio_snapshots column, performance_daily column
Confidence flags (lines 1118-1119) only in dashboard, not in table
Portfolio snapshots sharpe_ratio unexplained - conflicts with 252d rolling
Web clients cannot determine which sharpe to trust
252-day rolling sharpe in table but dashboard uses all-snapshots - DIFFERENT METHODOLOGY
findings.recommendation: Consolidate: fetch_perf() reads rolling_sharpe_252d from algo_performance_daily. Remove ad-hoc calculation from dashboard. Remove sharpe_ratio column from portfolio_snapshots (conflicting definition).
findings.table_status: algo_performance_daily.rolling_sharpe_252d (NUMERIC 8,4) exists; algo_portfolio_snapshots.sharpe_ratio (NUMERIC 8,4) also exists - CONFLICTING
findings.effort: HIGH - schema cleanup required; two tables have overlapping metrics

risk_if_left_alone: CALCULATED AT-THE-TIME in dashboard: depends on current snapshots available
Cannot recompute historical drawdown accurately (missing intraday data)
Portfolio snapshots max_drawdown_pct created daily but dashboard recalculates from memory
Web API has no way to serve historical max-drawdown curves
Intraday gaps invisible (line 1104 comment) but metric shown as fact
findings.recommendation: algo_performance_daily.max_drawdown_pct must be populated by loader with full equity history. Store as rolling max-drawdown(t) calculated from all snapshots since portfolio inception. Dashboard reads only.
findings.table_status: Column exists in algo_performance_daily and algo_portfolio_snapshots, but unknown population mechanism
findings.effort: HIGH - requires historical equity curve reconstruction in loader

findings.formula: mean(profit_loss_dollars for wins); mean(abs(profit_loss_dollars) for losses)
risk_if_left_alone: Web API must re-implement same logic
No handling for edge cases: what if no wins? (returns 0.0, not None)
Portfolio-level metric but computed per-call in dashboard; no caching
findings.recommendation: Add avg_win_pct_50t and avg_loss_pct_50t columns to algo_performance_daily (or use rolling window). Pre-calculate daily via loader.
findings.table_status: algo_performance_daily has avg_win_r_50t and avg_loss_r_50t (NUMERIC 6,3) - appears designed for 50-trade rolling window, NOT daily calculation
findings.effort: MEDIUM - columns partially exist; may need loader to populate

findings.logic: 'high' if snapshots>=20, 'medium' if 10-19, 'low' if <10, None if insufficient data
risk_if_left_alone: Business rule (20+ snapshots = high confidence) exists in Python only, not in database
Web API cannot communicate confidence to clients without duplicating logic
If threshold changes, must update dashboard + all web clients + API docs
findings.recommendation: Add sharpe_confidence_flag VARCHAR(10) to algo_performance_daily. Populate in loader based on snapshot count. Pass to clients as metadata.
findings.table_status: No such column exists; pure dashboard logic
findings.effort: LOW - straightforward metadata column; no complex calculation

risk_if_left_alone: Same as sharpe_confidence: business rule in Python, not DB
Breakeven threshold (5%, 15%) hardcoded in fetch_perf; if adjusted, must update web clients
Dashboard shows wr_confidence but returns as {'wr_confidence': value} - web clients may expect structured confidence metadata
findings.recommendation: Add wr_confidence_flag and breakeven_pct columns to algo_performance_daily. Define thresholds in database constraints or loader logic.
findings.table_status: No columns exist; fetch_perf() returns wr_confidence but as side-effect of calculation
findings.effort: LOW - metadata columns; coordinate with win_rate threshold changes

findings.logic: Iterates reversed(trades), counts consecutive wins or losses
risk_if_left_alone: Computed fresh on every fetch; no caching
Cannot determine if streak is 'hot hand' or normal variance without historical context
Web API clients need this for real-time dashboard; must call fetch_perf() every second
findings.recommendation: Add streak_current_wins INT and streak_current_losses INT to algo_performance_daily. Update daily in loader.
findings.table_status: No such columns; pure dashboard logic
findings.effort: MEDIUM - requires trade ordering verification in loader

findings.logic: Last 7 calendar days from snapshots; confidence based on count
risk_if_left_alone: Calendar days vs trading days mismatch (line 1146 comment): gaps over weekends invisible but metric shown
Cannot be replayed historically - last 7 days changes daily
Web clients need this for 1-week performance chart; must fetch entire portfolio history
findings.recommendation: Add recent_return_7d_pct and recent_return_30d_pct to algo_performance_daily. Computed daily in loader from snapshot history.
findings.table_status: No pre-calculated columns; would require new columns in performance_daily
findings.effort: MEDIUM - requires snapshot aggregation in loader

findings.should_be: table - economic_data (as derived column or separate table)
risk_if_left_alone: Dashboard computes YoY for display; API clients must do same
Requires two separate queries (current + 365d ago); could miss data if no historical point at exactly 365d
No caching: recalculates on every fetch despite daily data
findings.recommendation: Add cpi_yoy_pct column to economic_data when CPIAUCSL row is inserted. Computed in loader.
findings.table_status: economic_data.value stores raw CPI; no YoY column exists
findings.effort: LOW - simple derived column in loader

findings.should_be: table - price_daily (as derived column OR separate daily_changes table)
risk_if_left_alone: Requires two price_daily rows; if first row missing, calculation fails silently
Web API for 'market overview' must duplicate this logic
No caching; recalculates every fetch
findings.recommendation: Add price_change_pct column to price_daily when each day's close is recorded. Computed in price loader.
findings.table_status: price_daily stores only price, date, volume; no derived price_change_pct
findings.effort: LOW - straightforward derived column

findings.should_be: table - economic_data (derived columns or separate yield_curve table)
risk_if_left_alone: Requires three separate series lookups; dashboard does this each call
Web API clients need yield curve slope for market analysis; must call economic_pulse endpoint
No validation that all three series exist before subtraction
findings.recommendation: Add yc_10_2_slope and yc_10_3m_slope columns to economic_data or create separate yield_curve_daily table. Compute in fred_loader.
findings.table_status: economic_data stores raw series; no derived slope columns
findings.effort: MEDIUM - requires three-series join or new table

findings.should_be: Mostly good (in SQL), BUT should be stored in algo_positions table for easy API access
risk_if_left_alone: Currently computed on-the-fly in SELECT; no caching
If web API needs positions, must either: (a) call fetch_positions() or (b) re-query with same formula
Opportunity cost: computing per-call vs once-daily in algo_positions
findings.recommendation: Keep in SELECT (good design), but document that algo_positions table should mirror this. OR: create materialized view for positions + current_pnl.
findings.table_status: Computed in SELECT; not stored. algo_positions table likely exists but may drift from true state (algo_trades).
findings.effort: LOW - already in SQL, just needs documentation

findings.should_be: Good in SQL; could also be in algo_positions for API
risk_if_left_alone: Recalculated on every positions fetch (entry_time never changes)
Minor inefficiency; no business logic risk
findings.recommendation: Leave as-is; low impact. Could be pre-computed in loader if positions cache becomes important.
findings.table_status: Computed in SQL CTE; entry_time stored in algo_trades
findings.effort: NONE - working as intended

risk_if_left_alone: Performance metric but computed ad-hoc in dashboard
Web clients need 'average R per trade' to compare strategies; must call fetch_perf()
No rolling window logic; uses all-time average
findings.recommendation: Add avg_r_multiple_50t and avg_r_multiple_all to algo_performance_daily. Populate in loader.
findings.table_status: No columns exist; pure dashboard calculation
findings.effort: MEDIUM - straightforward mean calculation

findings.logic: Exposure pct → tier (confirmed_uptrend, healthy_uptrend, pressure, caution, correction)
risk_if_left_alone: Business logic (TIER_THRESHOLD_*) hardcoded in dashboard
Thresholds (lines 129-132) if changed, must update dashboard + all clients
Web API cannot easily return market tier without duplicating Python logic
findings.recommendation: Add market_tier VARCHAR(20) column to market_exposure_daily. Compute in exposure loader based on exposure_pct.
findings.table_status: No tier column; pure Python logic
findings.effort: LOW - simple if-else classification

findings.metric_name: Swing Score Grading Distribution (grades: A, B, C, D)
risk_if_left_alone: Computed via SQL but only in dashboard; not cached
Web clients need grade distribution for 'signal quality radar'; must query live
Historical grade distribution curves impossible without logging daily snapshots
findings.recommendation: Create signal_grade_distribution_daily table. Populate daily via loader with A/B/C/D counts and distribution. Dashboard reads from table.
findings.table_status: No such table; query happens at view time only
findings.effort: MEDIUM - new table + loader required

findings.logic: Fetches current rank and historical ranks; client computes delta
findings.should_be: Good design - rank deltas computed in table, not in dashboard
risk_if_left_alone: Actually well-designed: historical ranks pre-computed in loader
findings.recommendation: Keep as-is. Reference as example for other metrics.
findings.table_status: sector_ranking stores historical ranks; good practice
findings.effort: NONE - working well

risk_if_left_alone: Table defined in schema but NO LOADER found in loaders/
fetch_perf_analytics() reads from this table (line 1720) but table may be stale or empty
Dashboard cannot distinguish 'no data' from 'loader not run'
Web API would receive empty/NULL values for all performance metrics if table unpopulated
findings.recommendation: URGENT: Create loaders/load_algo_performance_daily.py to compute metrics from algo_trades and algo_portfolio_snapshots daily. Populate all columns listed above. Add unit tests.
findings.table_status: CRITICAL GAP: Table exists but no loader confirmed
findings.effort: HIGH - requires complex performance metric computation

findings.should_be: Populated daily (ideally multiple times per day for intraday context)
risk_if_left_alone: No confirmed loader populating this table
fetch_perf() reads from it (line 1092) for performance calculations
If unpopulated, all sharpe/drawdown/return metrics fail silently
Web API cannot return historical portfolio curves without this table
findings.recommendation: Ensure loaders/load_portfolio_snapshots.py exists and is called daily (should be part of reconciliation phase). Populate on each sync with Alpaca.
findings.table_status: CRITICAL GAP: Table schema exists but loader status unknown
findings.effort: HIGH - already partially implemented but needs verification and reliability hardening

risk_if_left_alone: Three different sharpe calculations with no indication of which is correct
Web clients asking 'what is sharpe ratio' have three possible answers
If one loader updates but not the other, values drift silently
Portfolio snapshots sharpe_ratio purpose unclear: day-specific sharpe? Daily? Rolling?
findings.recommendation: SCHEMA CLEANUP REQUIRED: (1) Remove sharpe_ratio column from algo_portfolio_snapshots. (2) Keep rolling_sharpe_252d in algo_performance_daily as single source. (3) Remove sharpe calculation from fetch_perf().
findings.table_status: CONFLICTING DEFINITIONS in two tables
findings.effort: MEDIUM - schema migration + fetcher updates

findings.metric_name: Missing Pre-Calculated Columns: Web API Readiness Gap
risk_if_left_alone: Web API endpoints must either: (a) call dashboard functions (circular dependency) or (b) duplicate calculation logic
Every new web feature requires re-implementing dashboard calculations
Mobile app will diverge from web if it implements logic differently
Metric definitions scattered across loaders and dashboard; no single source of truth
findings.recommendation: Systematically pre-calculate derived metrics in loaders. Update table schemas to include confidence flags and metadata columns. Dashboard reads from tables.
findings.table_status: Schema incomplete; missing columns for confidence, tiers, derived metrics
findings.effort: HIGH - requires systematic schema updates + loader enhancements

examples: sharpe_confidence based on current snapshot count (can't recompute for past date)
streak calculation uses reversed(trades) which is ever-changing
Days-since-entry recalculates daily (correct, but shows why position metrics need caching)
findings.should_be: Store snapshot/calculation at point-in-time; allow historical queries
risk_if_left_alone: Cannot answer: 'What was the 7-day return on 2026-05-15?'
Cannot replay dashboards from past dates with same data
Web API cannot serve historical performance curves; only current state
Backtests vs live trading diverge because calculations are point-in-time only
findings.recommendation: All daily metrics must be stored in algo_performance_daily at report_date, not computed on read. Treat snapshot_date as a point-in-time snapshot of current state.
findings.table_status: Schema allows this (report_date PRIMARY KEY) but not implemented
findings.effort: HIGH - architectural change; requires loader redesign

blockers: algo_performance_daily table exists in schema but no loader confirmed; table may be empty
Performance metrics scattered between dashboard calculations and tables; no single API source
Confidence flags, derived metrics, tiers exist in Python only, not in database schema
Cannot historically replay calculations (calculations depend on 'now')
minimum_effort_to_extend: Verify and fix algo_performance_daily and algo_portfolio_snapshots loaders (CRITICAL)
Add missing columns for confidence flags and derived metrics to tables
Remove performance calculations from fetch_perf(); read from table only
Consolidate sharpe ratio definitions (remove duplicates from portfolio_snapshots)
Pre-calculate all derived metrics (CPI YoY, yield curve slope, market tier, etc.) in loaders

red_flags.description: algo_performance_daily and algo_portfolio_snapshots tables exist in schema but no confirmed loaders populate them. fetch_perf_analytics() reads from potentially empty table.
red_flags.impact: CRITICAL - Web API will fail or return empty data if tables not populated

red_flags.description: fetch_perf() calculates expectancy; fetch_perf_analytics() reads from algo_performance_daily. If only one is maintained, values diverge.
red_flags.impact: CRITICAL - Web clients get inconsistent values

red_flags.description: algo_portfolio_snapshots.sharpe_ratio, algo_performance_daily.rolling_sharpe_252d, and fetch_perf() calculation three different values. No indication which is correct.
red_flags.impact: CRITICAL - Metric confusion; impossible to know authoritative value

red_flags.description: Thresholds, confidence rules, and calculations (win rate, profit factor, sharpe, etc.) implemented in Python, not database. If changed, must update dashboard + all clients.
red_flags.impact: HIGH - Inconsistency between platforms; maintenance burden

red_flags.description: Metrics like 'recent_rets', 'sharpe_confidence', and 'streak' computed based on current state. Cannot be replayed for historical dates.
red_flags.impact: HIGH - Cannot build historical performance curves; backtesting diverges from live trading

red_flags.description: fetch_perf() mixes data fetching (trades/snapshots), calculation (win rate, profit factor, sharpe), and business logic (confidence flags, breakeven handling). Hard to separate for web API.
red_flags.impact: MEDIUM - Code maintainability; difficult to reuse in web services

recommendations_priority.title: CRITICAL: Verify and Fix Performance Metrics Loaders
recommendations_priority.description: algo_performance_daily and algo_portfolio_snapshots tables exist but loader status unknown. If empty, entire web API will fail.
recommendations_priority.action: Search codebase for loaders populating these tables. If missing, create immediately. If present but not running, add to orchestrator schedule.

recommendations_priority.title: CRITICAL: Remove Sharpe Ratio Duplication
recommendations_priority.description: Three conflicting sharpe sources. Define single source of truth.
recommendations_priority.action: 1) Remove sharpe_ratio from algo_portfolio_snapshots. 2) Keep rolling_sharpe_252d in algo_performance_daily. 3) Remove sharpe calculation from fetch_perf().

recommendations_priority.title: CRITICAL: Consolidate Performance Metrics Calculations
recommendations_priority.description: fetch_perf() should read from algo_performance_daily, not calculate in Python. Currently duplicates expectancy, sharpe, and drawdown calculations.
recommendations_priority.action: Refactor fetch_perf() to: (1) Read pre-calculated metrics from algo_performance_daily. (2) Keep trade/snapshot fetches for historical curves. (3) Remove all metric calculations from this function.

recommendations_priority.title: HIGH: Add Confidence Flags and Metadata to Schema
recommendations_priority.description: Business logic (confidence thresholds, tiers, derived metrics) hardcoded in dashboard. Move to database.
recommendations_priority.action: Add columns to tables: (1) algo_performance_daily: wr_confidence_flag, sharpe_confidence_flag, breakeven_pct. (2) market_exposure_daily: market_tier. (3) economic_data: cpi_yoy_pct, yc_slopes.

recommendations_priority.title: HIGH: Pre-Calculate All Derived Metrics in Loaders
recommendations_priority.description: Metrics like CPI YoY, yield curve slope, market tier currently computed in dashboard. Pre-calculate in loaders for API reuse.
recommendations_priority.action: Update loaders to compute and store derived metrics. Update dashboard to read from tables instead of computing.

recommendations_priority.title: MEDIUM: Store Point-in-Time Snapshots for Historical Replay
recommendations_priority.description: Current metrics depend on 'now'. Cannot replay dashboards or build historical curves.
recommendations_priority.action: All daily metrics must be stored at report_date in algo_performance_daily, not computed on read. Include snapshot of all supporting metrics (snapshot count, recent_rets list, etc.) as JSON blob if needed.
files_to_change: tools/dashboard/dashboard.py (fetch_perf - read from table with report_date)

recommendations_priority.title: MEDIUM: Create API-First Data Schemas for Web Extension
recommendations_priority.description: Schema currently optimized for dashboard queries. Add views/tables optimized for web API access.
recommendations_priority.action: Create public API views that expose pre-calculated metrics with confidence flags. Example: v_portfolio_performance_daily (performance_date, wr_pct, sharpe_252d, confidence_level, data_timestamp).

conclusion.summary: Dashboard.py has significant architectural issues that will block web version extension. Performance metrics should be pre-calculated in dedicated tables (algo_performance_daily, algo_portfolio_snapshots) but current status suggests these tables may not be populated by loaders. Critical issues include: (1) Missing or non-functional performance loaders, (2) Duplication of expectancy and sharpe calculations between dashboard and table, (3) Business logic hardcoded in Python instead of database, (4) Metrics computed at render-time instead of stored at point-in-time, (5) Confidence flags and derived metrics missing from schema.
conclusion.minimum_work_required: 4-6 weeks: Fix loaders (2w), consolidate duplicates (1w), pre-calculate metrics (2w), schema cleanup (1w)
blocking_items: Fix algo_performance_daily and algo_portfolio_snapshots loaders - BLOCKER
Move metric calculations from dashboard to loaders - BLOCKER
